etymology.description: semantic 亻(亻) (rén, "person") + phonetic 兒(兒) (ér) -> limit
etymology.interpretation: The character 倪(ní, "limit") combines the semantic element 亻(亻) (rén, "person") which suggests a role or action involving people, with the phonetic element 兒(兒) (ér), indicating the sound. Originally, 倪 referred to the 'end or limit' of something in metaphoric comparison to the limits or boundaries associated with human affairs or abilities.

examples.chinese: 我们尚未达到能力的极限(我們尚未達到能力的極限)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen shàng wèi dádào nénglì de jíxiàn.
examples.english: We have not yet reached the limits of our abilities.

examples.chinese: 他对未来毫无恐惧，因为相信没有边际(他對未來毫無恐懼，因為相信沒有邊際)。
examples.pinyin: Tā duì wèilái háo wú kǒngjù, yīnwèi xiāngxìn méiyǒu biānjì.
examples.english: He has no fear of the future, as he believes there are no boundaries.

examples.chinese: 许多科学家试图理解宇宙的终极极限(許多科學家試圖理解宇宙的終極極限)。
examples.pinyin: Xǔduō kēxuéjiā shìtú lǐjiě yǔzhòu de zhōngjí jíxiàn.
examples.english: Many scientists attempt to understand the ultimate limits of the universe.
